figures.caption: Fig. 2. Qualitative comparison under the controlled protocol. (a) LoveDA: Urban/Rural examples illustrating boundary delineation and robustness under domain shift. (b) ISPRS Potsdam: fine-grained structures (buildings, impervious surfaces, vegetation, cars) in very-high-resolution aerial imagery.
Fig. 3. Efficiency analysis on LoveDA: A comparison of mIoU versus FPS across VSS backbones.
TABLE I CONTROLLED BENCHMARK RESULTS ON LOVEDA AND ISPRS POTSDAM. U→R AND R→U DENOTE SOURCE-ONLY CROSS-DOMAIN MIOU. TOP-3 PER COLUMN: 1ST, 2ND, 3RD.

Fig. 1. Overview of the controlled benchmark pipeline. To ensure fair comparison, all backbones including visual SSMs (VMamba, MambaVision, SpatialMamba) and baselines (CNNs, Transformers) are interfaced through a unified 4-stage feature pyramid and evaluated with the same lightweight U-Net decoder under an identical training and evaluation protocol.
